channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What the Supreme Court did on the final day of its term
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the right of children born on U.S. soil to automatic American citizenship. In so doing, the court rejected President Trump's most aggressive attempt to limit immigration.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court upheld the right of children born on U.S. soil to automatic American citizenship. In so doing, the court rejected President Trump's most aggressive attempt to limit immigration.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Belfast violence: What to know about the fascist youth groups known as 'active clubs'
item.description: Racist mob violence in Northern Ireland earlier this month has drawn keen interest from extremist groups and figures in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: Racist mob violence in Northern Ireland earlier this month has drawn keen interest from extremist groups and figures in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: Footballers donate socks to hooves in need
item.description: The U.K. football club Arsenal have donated dozens of socks — to a donkey and horse sanctuary.
item.content:encoded: The U.K. football club Arsenal have donated dozens of socks — to a donkey and horse sanctuary.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: What we can take away from the Supreme Court's decision on birthright citizenship
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with University of Virginia law professor Amanda Frost about the Supreme Court decision rejecting President Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with University of Virginia law professor Amanda Frost about the Supreme Court decision rejecting President Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Megan Lim

item.title: NPR discusses error in reporting on the last day of the Supreme Court term
item.description: All Things Considered host Scott Detrow speaks with NPR's editor-in-chief Thomas Evans and Nina Totenberg about her reporting on the final day of the Supreme Court term.
item.content:encoded: All Things Considered host Scott Detrow speaks with NPR's editor-in-chief Thomas Evans and Nina Totenberg about her reporting on the final day of the Supreme Court term.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Life Kit: How to handle the heat wave
item.description: Extreme heat will blanket a majority of American states through the July 4 weekend, according to forecasters. Learn how to keep cool, hydrated and safe in soaring temperatures.
item.content:encoded: Extreme heat will blanket a majority of American states through the July 4 weekend, according to forecasters. Learn how to keep cool, hydrated and safe in soaring temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Longtime public servant 'guardedly patriotic' as America turns 250
item.description: At age 72, former Houston-area county commissioner Rodney Ellis has seen a lot of both progress and setbacks for America. He's celebrating America's birthday, but has concerns about her future.
item.content:encoded: At age 72, former Houston-area county commissioner Rodney Ellis has seen a lot of both progress and setbacks for America. He's celebrating America's birthday, but has concerns about her future.
item.dc:creator: John Burnett

item.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
item.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
item.content:encoded: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey

item.title: Jenny Jackson's new book is about friends coming of age in middle age
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with author Jenny Jackson about her new novel The Shampoo Effect.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with author Jenny Jackson about her new novel The Shampoo Effect.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Dorning

item.title: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
item.description: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
item.content:encoded: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
